prompt: Using plotnine and the mtcars dataset (from plotnine.data import mtcars; import pandas as pd; df = pd.DataFrame(mtcars)), create a scatter plot of horsepower (hp) on x and miles per gallon (mpg) on y. Color points by number of cylinders treated as a categorical variable. Add a linear trend line with no confidence interval. Apply theme_minimal and add descriptive axis labels. Save the plot to 'output.png'.

Using plotnine and mtcars (from plotnine.data import mtcars; import pandas as pd; df = pd.DataFrame(mtcars)), create a bar chart showing count of cars for each cylinder count (cyl as factor). Add percentage labels above each bar using geom_text with after_stat and format_string. Use theme_classic. Save to 'output.png'.

Using plotnine and mtcars (from plotnine.data import mtcars; import pandas as pd; df = pd.DataFrame(mtcars)), create a boxplot of miles per gallon (mpg) grouped by cylinder count (cyl as factor). Facet the plot by the number of gears (gear as factor) using facet_wrap. Apply theme_light and add a title. Save to 'output.png'.

Load 'example_datasets/Social_media_impact_on_life.csv' into a pandas DataFrame. Using plotnine, create a histogram of 'Avg_Daily_Usage_Hours' filled by 'Gender' with 20 bins and alpha=0.7. Facet by 'Academic_Level'. Apply theme_bw and add a descriptive title and axis labels with labs(). Save to 'output.png'.

Using plotnine and mtcars (from plotnine.data import mtcars; import pandas as pd; df = pd.DataFrame(mtcars)), create a boxplot of miles per gallon (mpg) grouped by cylinder count (cyl as factor). Overlay a jitter layer (alpha=0.4, width=0.15) to show individual points. Color boxes and points by cyl factor. Use theme_minimal. Save to 'output.png'.

Load 'example_datasets/Social_media_impact_on_life.csv'. Group by 'Age' and compute the mean of 'Mental_Health_Score'. Using plotnine, create a line chart with age on x and mean score on y. Overlay point markers. Color the line 'steelblue'. Apply theme_minimal and add axis labels. Save to 'output.png'.

Using plotnine and mtcars (from plotnine.data import mtcars; import pandas as pd; df = pd.DataFrame(mtcars)), create a scatter plot of wt vs mpg. Apply theme_bw, then use theme() to: hide the legend, make axis titles bold (element_text face='bold'), and remove the panel grid minor lines (element_blank). Save to 'output.png'.
